description:
You are a molecular biologist designing a recombinant expression construct to clone the human erythropoietin (EPO) coding sequence into a pET-28a(+) expression vector for bacterial protein production.

Input files at ~/UGENE_Data/cloning_design/:
- pET28a_vector.gb — pET-28a(+) expression vector, 5369 bp, circular
- epo_insert.gb — human EPO coding sequence, 582 bp, linear

Analyze both sequences in UGENE. For the vector, find all ORFs (minimum 300 bp) and map all restriction enzyme sites. For the EPO insert, map all restriction enzyme sites. Identify a compatible pair of two different restriction enzymes that each cut exactly once within the pET-28a multiple cloning site (MCS, positions 158–203) and do not cut anywhere within the EPO sequence.

Export the annotated vector to ~/UGENE_Data/cloning_design/results/pET28a_annotated.gb and the annotated EPO insert to ~/UGENE_Data/cloning_design/results/epo_annotated.gb, both in GenBank format with restriction site and ORF annotations preserved. Generate a circular map of the annotated vector and export it as ~/UGENE_Data/cloning_design/results/vector_map.svg.

Write a cloning strategy report to ~/UGENE_Data/cloning_design/results/cloning_report.txt containing: the selected enzyme pair with their cut positions in the vector, confirmation that neither enzyme cuts in EPO, the expected recombinant construct size, and a complete list of all ORFs found in the vector with their names and base-pair positions.

metadata.data_source: Deterministic generation based on real pET-28a(+) MCS and human EPO CDS properties
